creator: freebsd@nagilum.org
text:
Just tested it.
It works with the standard compiler.
However I use gcc45 where possible setting this in /etc/make.conf:
CC=gcc45
CXX=g++45
CPP=cpp45

So it's only a nice-to-have thing but given that it's only a small  
thing that prevents this port from building with another CC you may  
want to get it right so that it works either way.
I'm currently testing:

INSTALLROOT=    ${WRKSRC}/arch/freebsd*.${ARCH}

Lets see if that works..

----- Message from sunpoet@FreeBSD.org ---------
     Date: Wed, 15 Jun 2011 01:13:37 +0800
     From: Sunpoet Po-Chuan Hsieh <sunpoet@FreeBSD.org>
  Subject: Re: ports/157680: shells/ksh93 fails to install
       To: freebsd@nagilum.org
       Cc: naddy@mips.inka.de, bug-followup@FreeBSD.org


> Hi,
>
>   The INSTALLROOT issue should be solved in Makefile r1.61. Please
>   'make clean' to remove your work directory of shells/ksh93 and
>   rebuild this port. I've tested it on FreeBSD 8.2-RELEASE i386. My
>   build log is at http://people.freebsd.org/~sunpoet/ksh93.i386.txt
>   Thanks.
>
> Regards,
> sunpoet
>
